title: Materials
description: Density, Hooke's law and elastic strain energy, tensile stress, strain and the Young modulus, and how stress-strain graphs distinguish brittle, ductile and polymeric material behaviour.
objectives: Calculate density from mass and volume, and rearrange the density equation
State Hooke's law and use F = kx up to the limit of proportionality
Distinguish elastic from plastic deformation, and calculate elastic strain energy from a force-extension graph
Define and calculate tensile stress, tensile strain and the Young modulus
Interpret a stress-strain graph, identifying the limit of proportionality, elastic limit, yield point, ultimate tensile stress and breaking stress
Compare the stress-strain behaviour of brittle, ductile and polymeric materials

lessons.title: Stress, strain and the Young modulus
